# Requirements Alignment Checklist: Tool Contract and Gateway Baseline - M2B.1

**Purpose**: Pressure-test the feature specification against SRS, roadmap, architecture, technical design, contracts, traceability, and constitution requirements before task generation.
**Created**: 2026-07-02
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [X] CHK001 Are all M2B.1 source requirements from SRS `FR-2.4.1` through `FR-2.4.6` represented by explicit functional requirements rather than only by governance references? [Completeness, Spec §Governance Context, Spec §Functional Requirements, SRS §FR-2.4]
- [X] CHK002 Are `FR-4.2.1`, `NFR-5.2.12`, `NFR-6.2.6`, `CON-6`, `CON-9`, `CON-10`, `AC-9.1` through `AC-9.4`, and `IR-3.1` through `IR-3.2` each mapped to at least one spec requirement or success criterion? [Traceability, Spec §Governance Context, Spec §Functional Requirements, SRS §Traceability Matrix]
- [X] CHK003 Does the spec define descriptor requirements for all baseline tools named by SRS and roadmap: `StockSymbolTool`, `TradingViewTool`, and `ReportingTool`? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §Key Entities, Roadmap §2B.1]
- [X] CHK004 Does the spec document disabled and non-exposed descriptor behavior for placeholder or scaffolded tools without implying runtime capability? [Completeness, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §User Story 1, Technical Design §3.2.1]
- [X] CHK005 Are route-filtered exposure requirements complete for all current static `StockQueryRoute` values named by the spec? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §Assumptions, Architecture §4.3.2]
- [X] CHK006 Are requirements present for routes with no admitted M2B.1 tool so that empty model-visible surfaces are intentional and not ambiguous gaps? [Coverage, Spec §M2B1-FR-015, Spec §Edge Cases, Contract §Route Fixture Expectations]
- [X] CHK007 Are gateway admission requirements complete for route-tool match, input arguments, risk class, license posture, descriptor integrity, timeout budget, and conditionally applicable provider/cache/freshness state? [Completeness, Spec §M2B1-FR-007, SRS §FR-2.4.4, Contract §Admission Inputs]
- [X] CHK008 Are trace requirements complete for required and conditionally applicable fields without requiring the later full `ToolExecutionEnvelope` or `ToolContextPack` backbone? [Completeness, Spec §M2B1-FR-012, Spec §M2B1-FR-018, SRS §NFR-5.2.12]
- [X] CHK009 Does the spec include enough public-surface boundary requirements to keep full gateway traces internal while allowing only safe degraded-state or warning metadata? [Completeness, Spec §M2B1-FR-017, System SRS §SR-5, SRS §NFR-5.2.12]

## Requirement Clarity

- [X] CHK010 Is the phrase "current model-visible tools" clarified against the actual baseline inventory and enabled/non-exposed states? [Clarity, Spec §M2B1-FR-001, Spec §Assumptions, Technical Design §3.2.1]
- [X] CHK011 Is "model-safe capability descriptor" defined with enough field-level inclusions and exclusions to avoid leaking internal policy or provider details? [Clarity, Spec §M2B1-FR-002, Spec §M2B1-FR-003, SRS §IR-3.1]
- [X] CHK012 Is "internal policy descriptor" defined with enough field-level inclusions to support gateway admission and descriptor integrity checks? [Clarity, Spec §M2B1-FR-004, SRS §IR-3.2, Contract §ToolPolicyDescriptor]
- [X] CHK013 Is "admitted risk class" defined or constrained enough for M2B.1 readers to distinguish non-mutating baseline behavior from later mutation-capable tool work? [Ambiguity, Spec §M2B1-FR-005, Spec §M2B1-FR-014, Constitution §Agent Tool System and Architecture Governance]
- [X] CHK014 Is "degraded state" specified as a machine-detectable outcome rather than a vague user-facing error phrase? [Clarity, Spec §Key Entities, Spec §M2B1-FR-008, Contract §Admission Output]
- [X] CHK015 Is "descriptor drift" distinguishable from missing descriptors, invalid descriptors, and unapproved remote descriptors? [Clarity, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §M2B1-FR-010, SRS §AC-9.4]
- [X] CHK016 Is "provider or cache state when applicable" clear enough to prevent implementers from treating provider-adapter readiness as mandatory M2B.1 scope? [Ambiguity, Spec §M2B1-FR-007, Spec §Assumptions, Roadmap §M2B.1]
- [X] CHK017 Is "safe warning metadata" bounded enough to prevent internal admission reasons, license posture, credentials, or provider fallback rules from becoming public API content? [Clarity, Spec §M2B1-FR-017, Spec §Contract Impact, SRS §NFR-4.2.3]

## Requirement Consistency

- [X] CHK018 Are the strict `TS-01` and `TS-02` scope statements consistent across the input, clarifications, functional requirements, success criteria, and assumptions? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §M2B1-FR-014, Spec §SC-010, Roadmap §M2B.1]
- [X] CHK019 Does the spec avoid contradicting the roadmap's broader immediate delivery slice by explicitly limiting this feature to the M2B.1 milestone gate? [Consistency, Spec §Input, Spec §Clarifications, Roadmap §Immediate Delivery Slice]
- [X] CHK020 Are the feature-local contracts consistent with the spec's promise not to define public REST, SSE, or WebSocket schema changes? [Consistency, Spec §Contract Impact, Spec §M2B1-FR-017, Contract §Scope]
- [X] CHK021 Are the route names used in scenarios, requirements, contracts, and independent tests consistent with the current static route taxonomy? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 2, Contract §Route Fixture Expectations, Architecture §4.3.2]
- [X] CHK022 Are placeholder statuses for `TradingViewTool` and scaffold statuses for `ReportingTool` consistent between spec, technical design, and route-surface contract? [Consistency, Spec §Assumptions, Contract §Baseline Tool Expectations, Technical Design §3.2.1]
- [X] CHK023 Are traceability targets consistent with the generated reverse trace statuses for the planned feature lifecycle? [Consistency, Spec §Traceability Target, SRS_SPEC_TRACEABILITY §FR-2, SRS_SPEC_TRACEABILITY §AC-9]
- [X] CHK024 Does the spec preserve the architecture boundary that `ToolGateway` is in-process and does not become provider parser, second runtime, service owner, or prompt-policy author? [Consistency, Spec §M2B1-FR-011, Architecture §4.3.1, Tools Proposal §1.3]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [X] CHK025 Are success criteria measurable for descriptor coverage, route filtering, disallowed calls, descriptor drift, trace completeness, secret exclusion, latency, and public-trace exclusion? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §Success Criteria]
- [X] CHK026 Can `SC-003` be objectively evaluated for every tested route without relying on subjective wording such as "expected existing tool families"? [Measurability, Spec §SC-003, Contract §Route Fixture Expectations]
- [X] CHK027 Is the `95%` trace-completeness threshold in `SC-006` clear about the denominator: governed tool runs, admitted runs, denied runs, or both? [Ambiguity, Spec §SC-006, SRS §NFR-5.2.12]
- [X] CHK028 Is the `50 ms` latency target in `SC-008` scoped clearly to surface construction plus gateway admission rather than provider execution or LLM latency? [Clarity, Spec §SC-008, Plan §Performance Goals]
- [X] CHK029 Are compatibility-regression outcomes clear about which user-visible changes are allowed: metadata, warnings, and degraded-state disclosures only? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-002, Roadmap §2B.1]
- [X] CHK030 Are public API and streaming fixtures in `SC-009` scoped as contract-safety checks rather than evidence of Socket.IO lifecycle parity closure? [Clarity, Spec §SC-009, Spec §M2B1-FR-016, Architecture Memory §Socket.IO parity gap]

## Scenario Coverage

- [X] CHK031 Are primary flows covered for descriptor inventory, route-filtered exposure, gateway admission, and runtime auditability? [Coverage, Spec §User Scenarios]
- [X] CHK032 Are alternate flows covered for disabled tools, unrelated tools, routes without admitted tools, optional locale/context absence, and baseline-safe general-chat exposure? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §Assumptions]
- [X] CHK033 Are exception flows covered for malformed descriptors, duplicate tool names, unknown routes, invalid arguments, blocked license posture, descriptor drift, and timeout-budget denial? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §M2B1-FR-007, Contract §Denial Conditions]
- [X] CHK034 Are recovery or degraded-state requirements clear enough for denied gateway calls without requiring later provider fallback or normalized-output work? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §M2B1-FR-008, Roadmap §M2B.1]
- [X] CHK035 Are public-surface scenarios sufficiently covered for REST, SSE, and WebSocket trace leakage boundaries without expanding verification into Socket.IO lifecycle parity? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 4, Spec §M2B1-FR-016, System SRS §SR-5]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [X] CHK036 Are edge cases for descriptor tampering, unapproved remote descriptors, and descriptor drift complete enough to support fail-closed exposure and execution behavior? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §M2B1-FR-010, SRS §AC-9.4]
- [X] CHK037 Are edge cases for cache entries and provider health clear that cache state is not market-data authority or conversation memory? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Constitution §Tool Contracts and Data Integrity]
- [X] CHK038 Are edge cases for generic web fetch, remote/MCP admission, symbol-store mutations, report persistence, and full `ToolContextPack` explicitly excluded rather than silently absent? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §M2B1-FR-014, Roadmap §M2B.1]
- [X] CHK039 Are edge cases for `TradingViewTool` clear that descriptors are required but visualization behavior and evidence classification are later-scope unless explicitly admitted? [Edge Case, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §Assumptions, SRS §CON-7]
- [X] CHK040 Are route ambiguity and `general_chat` fallback cases specified to avoid broadening financial-tool exposure as a shortcut? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Contract §Route Fixture Expectations]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [X] CHK041 Are observability requirements aligned with system-level diagnosability and agent-domain trace completeness without exposing sensitive content? [Non-Functional, Spec §M2B1-FR-012, System SRS §SNR-5.2.1, SRS §NFR-5.2.12]
- [X] CHK042 Are secret and sensitive-user-data exclusions aligned between system SRS, agent SRS, and spec trace requirements? [Security, Spec §M2B1-FR-013, System SRS §SNR-3.1.1, SRS §NFR-4.2.3]
- [X] CHK043 Are performance requirements for gateway admission scoped to non-provider tool-selection overhead and compatible with streaming first-token latency expectations? [Performance, Spec §SC-008, System SRS §SNR-1.1.2]
- [X] CHK044 Are maintainability and architecture-boundary requirements aligned with factory/registry ownership and single-runtime preservation? [Maintainability, Spec §M2B1-FR-011, System SRS §SNR-6.1.2, Architecture §4.2.2a]
- [X] CHK045 Are finance-domain safety expectations represented enough for tool exposure and trace metadata even though broader recommendation-guardrail work is outside M2B.1? [Safety, Spec §M2B1-FR-014, System SRS §SR-3.3.1, SRS §AC-9.17]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [X] CHK046 Are assumptions about the baseline inventory, current static route taxonomy, optional locale/context, and no public API change explicit enough to avoid implementation-time scope expansion? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [X] CHK047 Are dependencies on `ToolRegistry`, `CachingTool`, the current ReAct runtime, and feature-local contracts stated as compatibility constraints rather than mandatory rewrites? [Dependency, Spec §User Story 4, Plan §Technical Context]
- [X] CHK048 Are licensing and source-authority assumptions grounded in SRS constraints while still avoiding provider-policy implementation in M2B.1? [Dependency, Spec §M2B1-FR-007, Spec §M2B1-FR-014, SRS §CON-6]
- [X] CHK049 Are traceability and sync dependencies complete for `spec-traceability.yaml`, `spec-sync-status.md`, and `SRS_SPEC_TRACEABILITY.md` across planned, implemented, and verified lifecycle states? [Traceability, Spec §Traceability Target, Constitution §Delivery Sync Gates]
- [X] CHK050 Are architecture-gap assumptions explicit that this feature does not close Socket.IO lifecycle parity, provider normalization, prompt compiler activation, or remote tool admission? [Assumption, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §M2B1-FR-016, Architecture Memory §Open Risks]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [X] CHK051 Is there any unresolved ambiguity between the roadmap milestone gate `TS-01` to `TS-02` and the roadmap immediate delivery slice `TS-01` to `TS-05` that should be called out before task generation? [Ambiguity, Spec §Clarifications, Roadmap §Milestone Gates, Roadmap §Immediate Delivery Slice]
- [X] CHK052 Is there any conflict between requiring descriptor metadata for disabled tools and requiring no scaffolded tool exposure for unsupported routes? [Conflict, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §M2B1-FR-015]
- [X] CHK053 Is there any conflict between `CON-9` source attribution expectations and M2B.1's decision not to implement provider-backed normalized outputs or retained reports? [Conflict, Spec §M2B1-FR-014, SRS §CON-9]
- [X] CHK054 Is there any ambiguity about whether safe degraded-state public metadata is already supported by current public contracts or would require `docs/openapi.yaml` updates? [Ambiguity, Spec §Contract Impact, Spec §M2B1-FR-017, System SRS §SR-8]
- [X] CHK055 Is there any ambiguity about whether trace records are ephemeral runtime metadata, retained audit metadata, or both for M2B.1 planning? [Ambiguity, Spec §Key Entities, Spec §M2B1-FR-012, Constitution §Tool Contracts and Data Integrity]

## Notes

- Focus areas: SRS and system SRS alignment, roadmap scope boundaries, architecture and technical-design boundary preservation, contract consistency, traceability, and ambiguity detection.
- Depth: Standard reviewer checklist, suitable before task generation.
- Actor/timing: Author and peer reviewer during the planned feature phase.
- Completed on 2026-07-02 after pre-implementation fleet-review remediation.
- Coverage verification now explicitly includes the touched agent-core `>=80%` gate, the touched tool-layer `>=70%` gate, and the repo-wide `56%` non-regression baseline.
- Dependency guidance now distinguishes between US3 core gateway work, which may start after US1 descriptors exist, and final agent wiring, which depends on the US2 route-filtered surface outputs.
